steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Substitute the point into the circle equation
steps.content:
Since $(8, c)$ lies on the circle $(x-4)^2 + (y-7)^2 = 16$, substitute $x = 8$ and $y = c$:
$$(8-4)^2 + (c-7)^2 = 16$$
Simplify:
$$16 + (c-7)^2 = 16$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content:
Subtract 16 from both sides:
$$(c-7)^2 = 0$$
Take the square root:
$$c - 7 = 0$$
$$c = 7$$
